Shiv.texts: Shiva Purana, Linga Purana, Skanda Purana, Vijnana Bhairava Tantra, Rudrayamala Tantra, Shiva Sutras
Shiv.desc: Wisdom of the Auspicious One, the supreme ascetic, and destroyer of illusions

Vishnu.texts: Vishnu Purana, Bhagavata Purana, Naradiya Purana, Garuda Purana, Matsya Purana
Vishnu.desc: Wisdom of the Preserver, sustainer of Dharma, and supreme cosmic reality

Brahma.texts: Brahma Purana, Brahmanda Purana, Padma Purana (Srishti Khanda)
Brahma.desc: Wisdom of the Creator and source of the Vedas

Ganesh.texts: Ganesha Purana, Mudgala Purana, Ganapati Atharvashirsha, Ganesha Gita
Ganesh.desc: Remover of obstacles, lord of intellect, and patron of arts and sciences

Rama.texts: Valmiki Ramayana, Ramcharitmanas, Adhyatma Ramayana, Yoga Vasistha
Rama.desc: The 7th Avatar of Vishnu, embodiment of Dharma, and the perfect king

Krishna.texts: Bhagavata Purana, Harivamsa, Mahabharata, Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Vaivarta Purana
Krishna.desc: The 8th Avatar of Vishnu, supreme teacher, and divine statesman

Durga.texts: Devi Mahatmyam (Chandi Path), Devi Bhagavata Purana, Markandeya Purana, durga saptashati
Durga.desc: The invincible divine Mother's power and protector of the universe

Kali.texts: Kalika Purana, Mahanirvana Tantra, Karpuradi Stotra, Todala Tantra
Kali.desc: The fierce manifestation of time, creation, destruction, and power

Saraswati.texts: Saraswati Purana, Devi Bhagavata Purana, Saraswati Rahasya Upanishad
Saraswati.desc: Goddess of knowledge, wisdom, music, and the flowing word

Lakshmi.texts: Lakshmi Tantra, Sri Suktam, Vishnu Purana, Mahalakshmi Ashtakam
Lakshmi.desc: Goddess of wealth, fortune, purity, and spiritual prosperity

Hanuman.texts: Hanuman Chalisa, Sundara Kanda (Ramayana), Parashara Samhita, Muktika Upanishad
Hanuman.desc: The supreme epitome of devotion, strength, intellect, and selfless service

Surya.texts: Aditya Hrudayam, Surya Siddhanta, Samba Purana, Surya Mandala Stotram
Surya.desc: The dispeller of darkness, source of life, and the soul of the universe

Dattatreya.texts: Avadhuta Gita, Tripura Rahasya, Jivanmukta Gita, Dattatreya Upanishad
Dattatreya.desc: The supreme ascetic, cosmic guru, and combined avatar of the Trimurti

Kartikeya.texts: Skanda Purana, Tirumurugatruppadai, Kanda Puranam, Kumara Sambhava
Kartikeya.desc: The god of war, victory, wisdom, and commander of the celestial armies

Ayyappan.desc: The ascetic warrior god born of Shiva and Mohini (Vishnu), supreme in Kerala

Venkateshwara.texts: Venkatachala Mahatmya (Skanda Purana), Varaha Purana, Bhavishyottara Purana
Venkateshwara.desc: The supreme lord of Tirumala, presiding deity of the Kali Yuga

Jagannath.texts: Skanda Purana (Utkala Khanda), Niladri Mahodaya, Brahma Purana
Jagannath.desc: The Lord of the Universe and abstract form of Krishna worshipped in Puri

Vithoba.texts: Panduranga Mahatmya (Padma Purana), Abhangas of Varkari Saints
Vithoba.desc: The beloved manifestation of Krishna standing on a brick in Pandharpur

Narasimha.texts: Narasimha Purana, Bhagavata Purana, Vishnu Purana
Narasimha.desc: The fierce lion-man, 4th Avatar of Vishnu, protector of devotees

Parashurama.texts: Brahmanda Purana (Bhargava Charita), Mahabharata, Kalika Purana
Parashurama.desc: The 6th Avatar of Vishnu, the warrior sage who cleansed the earth

Khandoba.texts: Malhari Mahatmya (Brahmanda Purana), Folk traditions of Deccan
Khandoba.desc: The fierce warrior manifestation of Shiva revered in Maharashtra and Deccan

Bhavani.texts: Devi Mahatmyam, Tulja Bhavani Mahatmya, Skanda Purana
Bhavani.desc: The fierce, protecting mother goddess and patron deity of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj

Kamakhya.texts: Kalika Purana, Yogini Tantra, Hevajra Tantra
Kamakhya.desc: The supreme bleeding goddess of desire and the most powerful Shakti Peetha

Meenakshi.desc: The fish-eyed warrior goddess of Madurai, a manifestation of Parvati

Mariamman.texts: Tamil Agamas, Karaneekam, Regional Sangam lore
Mariamman.desc: The ancient South Indian goddess of rain, medicine, and protection from disease

Indra.desc: The Vedic King of the Devas, lord of storms, lightning, and rain

Agni.texts: Rig Veda, Agni Purana, Taittiriya Samhita
Agni.desc: The Vedic god of fire, the acceptor of sacrifices, and messenger of the gods

Varuna.desc: The Vedic god of the oceans, cosmic order (Rita), and justice

Yama.texts: Katha Upanishad, Garuda Purana, Rig Veda
Yama.desc: The lord of death, dharma, and the ultimate dispenser of justice

Vishwakarma.texts: Vishwakarma Purana, Vastu Shastras, Rig Veda
Vishwakarma.desc: The divine architect, craftsman, and creator of the universe's mechanics

Shani.texts: Skanda Purana (Kashi Khanda), Shani Mahatmya, Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra
Shani.desc: The lord of karma, discipline, and the most feared and respected planetary deity

Dhanvantari.texts: Sushruta Samhita, Bhagavata Purana, Brahmanda Purana
Dhanvantari.desc: The physician of the gods and the divine source of Ayurveda

Kamadeva.texts: Shiva Purana, Kama Sutra, Atharva Veda
Kamadeva.desc: The Vedic god of human love, desire, and passion
